title: zannichellia palustris subsp. marina
intro: Zannichellia palustris subsp. marina, also known as sea ash or saltwort, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Zannichelliaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of sea ash include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health. Sea ash can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, and is often used to treat various skin issues and respiratory problems.
